mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in > 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized demand contraction that suppresses corporate earnings growth and produces negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint decline periods show positive 60d returns in > 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production index increase above 0.5% during a monetary tightening cycle reflects robust output growth that outweighs rate hike concerns, generating positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are negative in > 50% of high-INDPRO months during tightening.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 220.0 indicates accelerating price pressures that force central banks to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative monthly returns.
failure_condition: 20d returns are positive in > 65% of months above threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly nonfarm payroll growth above 200000 signals expanding economic capacity and rising corporate revenues, which mechanically supports positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly returns are negative in > 55% of high-PAYEMS months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly consumption expenditures reveals underlying demand fragility that triggers risk-off positioning and negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Divergence periods yield positive 20d returns in > 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous monthly decline in industrial production and housing starts below their 6-month moving averages signals a supply-side contraction that reduces near-term earnings expectations and drives negative weekly returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both below 6-month MA
failure_condition: Weekly returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances.
